---
title: "semiring_conv"
generated: true
description: "Semiring-generalised convolution."
---

[‚Äπ back to `nitrix.semiring.conv`](../nitrix-semiring-conv.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:semiring_conv -->
```python
semiring_conv(x: Num[Array, '... *spatial c_in'], k: Num[Array, '*kspatial c_in c_out'], *, semiring: Semiring[Any] = REAL, stride: Union[int, Sequence[int]] = 1, padding: Union[str, Sequence[tuple[int, int]]] = 'SAME', dilation: Union[int, Sequence[int]] = 1, backend: Backend = 'auto') -> Num[Array, '... *spatial_out c_out']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:semiring_conv -->
Semiring-generalised convolution.

Channel-last layout: `x: (..., *spatial, c_in)`,
`k: (*kspatial, c_in, c_out)`.  Leading batch dims are
arbitrary; the convolution slides `k` over `x` along the
spatial dims and reduces via the supplied `semiring`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:semiring_conv -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `x` | `Num` | `...` √ó `*spatial` √ó `c_in` | ‚Äî | Input array, `(..., *spatial, c_in)`. |
| `k` | `Num` | `*kspatial` √ó `c_in` √ó `c_out` | ‚Äî | Kernel, `(*kspatial, c_in, c_out)`. The number of trailing spatial dims of `x` is inferred from `k.ndim - 2`. |
| `semiring` | `Semiring[Any]` | ‚Äî | `REAL` | Algebra to reduce under. Defaults to [`REAL`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd) (i.e., standard linear convolution). |
| `stride` | `Union[int, Sequence[int]]` | ‚Äî | `1` | Per-spatial-dim stride. Either an `int` (applied to every spatial dim) or a per-dim sequence. |
| `padding` | `Union[str, Sequence[tuple[int, int]]]` | ‚Äî | `'SAME'` | `"SAME"`, `"VALID"`, or an explicit per-spatial-dim sequence of `(lo, hi)` pairs. |
| `dilation` | `Union[int, Sequence[int]]` | ‚Äî | `1` | Per-spatial-dim kernel dilation (rhs_dilation in lax terms). |
| `backend` | `Backend` | ‚Äî | `'auto'` | `"auto"`, `"pallas-cuda"`, or `"jax"`. At first GA there is no native Pallas conv kernel; `"pallas-cuda"` is accepted (so callers don't have to special-case conv) but always falls back to the JAX patches path with a warning. |

**Returns** `Num` ‚Äî shape `...` √ó `*spatial_out` √ó `c_out`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:semiring_conv -->
::: {.callout-note}
The forward path reuses [`semiring_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)
over reshaped patches, so:

- `backend` is honoured for the inner reduction: `pallas-cuda`
  uses the native Pallas matmul kernel for the per-output-position
  reduction.
- The backward composes through
  [`semiring_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)'s registered VJP
  automatically; no per-algebra `conv_vjp` is required.

For [`REAL`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd), this produces the same answer as
`lax.conv_general_dilated` modulo TF32 vs strict-fp32 precision
(see [`semiring_matmul`](../nitrix-semiring.qmd)'s docstring).
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:semiring_conv -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
| `c_in` | *(undocumented ‚Äî add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `*kspatial` | a variadic group of axes (`kspatial`) |
| `c_out` | *(undocumented ‚Äî add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `*spatial_out` | a variadic group of axes (`spatial_out`) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
